title: Determine Tipper Or Station Status From Operator Station Stacklights
summary: Use the documented operator station stacklight color, flashing pattern, and horn pattern to determine the current status of the corresponding tipper and station.
when_to_use: Use when an operator needs to identify the documented current status of the corresponding tipper or station by observing the operator station stacklights and any associated horn pattern.
not_for: Do not use to infer corrective actions when the source provides status meaning only.
Do not use if the observed stacklight or horn pattern does not match any documented indication.

responsibilities: Observe the operator station stacklights and horn pattern.
Compare the observed indication to the documented mapping.
Record or communicate the documented status meaning for the corresponding tipper or station.

safety_notes: This runbook is a status interpretation reference only.
Do not infer corrective actions from the status mapping when the source provides status meaning only.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to the operator station stacklights
Ability to observe horn alarm presence and pattern

access_required: Visual access to the operator station stacklights
system_preconditions: The operator station stacklights are visible to the user.
The user can observe whether the indication is solid or flashing and whether a horn alarm is present.
external_references: OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual, operator station stacklight status mapping
Figure 4-28 Operator Station HMI Alarms Screen

visual_references.description: Photo reference for stacklight hardware appearance.
Operator Station HMI VISU_ALARM screen showing tipper alarms and timestamps; useful when checking alarm context alongside stacklight and horn indications.
Operator station HMI screen referenced in packet as related operator station interface context.
Operator station HMI data screen referenced in packet as related operator station interface context.

steps.title: Locate the operator station stacklights
steps.purpose: Identify the physical indicators used to determine the current tipper or station status.
steps.instruction: Locate the operator station stacklights at the top of either side of the frame.
steps.expected_result: The two operator station stacklights are identified for observation.
steps.healthy_condition: The stacklights are visible and can be observed clearly.
steps.failure_condition: The stacklights cannot be located or cannot be observed clearly.

actions.label: Locate stacklights
actions.exact_action: Locate the operator station stacklights at the top of either side of the frame.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Physical appearance and location style of the stacklight assembly.

stop_or_escalate_if: The stacklights are obstructed or not visible enough to interpret.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Operator stations have two stacklights located at the top of either side of the frame.

steps.title: Determine whether the indication is solid or flashing
steps.purpose: Separate the observed indication into the documented solid or flashing categories before matching status.
steps.instruction: Observe whether the displayed indication is solid or flashing.
steps.expected_result: The indication is classified as either solid or flashing.
steps.healthy_condition: The light pattern is clear enough to distinguish steady illumination from flashing.
steps.failure_condition: The light pattern cannot be reliably distinguished as solid or flashing.

actions.label: Observe light pattern
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the displayed indication is solid or flashing.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The illuminated stacklight section and whether it appears steady or flashing.

stop_or_escalate_if: The indication cannot be reliably determined as solid or flashing.
The observed pattern appears inconsistent with the documented categories.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The stacklights provide information about the current status of the corresponding tipper and station, with listed solid and flashing color meanings and horn patterns.

steps.title: Identify the color pattern and any horn alarm
steps.purpose: Capture the full observed indication needed to match the documented status mapping.
steps.instruction: Identify the displayed color or color combination and note whether a horn alarm is present and whether it is long or intermittent.
steps.expected_result: The observed color or color combination and horn pattern are identified.
steps.healthy_condition: The user can state the observed color pattern and whether a horn alarm is present, long, or intermittent.
steps.failure_condition: The color pattern or horn pattern cannot be determined confidently.

actions.label: Identify color and horn pattern
actions.exact_action: Identify the displayed color or color combination and note whether a horn alarm is present and whether it is long or intermittent.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_ALARM view showing tipper alarms and timestamps if alarm context needs to be reviewed alongside the observed horn or stacklight indication.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed color or color combination is unclear.
A horn alarm is heard but its pattern cannot be determined.
The observed indication does not appear to match a documented color or horn combination.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Operator station stacklights provide information about the current status of the corresponding tipper and station, with listed solid and flashing color meanings and horn patterns.
The Visu_Alarm screen displays a list of all tipper alarms along with a timestamp.

steps.title: Match the observed indication to the documented status mapping
steps.purpose: Translate the observed stacklight and horn pattern into the documented tipper or station status.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed indication to the documented mappings: Solid Blue = tipper is paused; Solid Green = tipping in process; Solid Red = Estop is activated; Solid White = AGV is present (post-tipping); Solid Red, Yellow, Green = RMS error; Flashing Blue = tipper is disabled; Flashing Green = tipper is "starved"; Flashing Red = tipper is faulted; Flashing White = SurePost tote is being sent to the station; Flashing Yellow = AGV is present (pre-tipping); Flashing Green, Yellow, Green with long horn alarm = sort is starting; Flashing White with intermittent horn alarm = bagout is complete.
steps.expected_result: A documented status meaning is assigned to the observed indication.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed indication matches one documented mapping exactly.
steps.failure_condition: The observed indication does not match any documented mapping exactly.

actions.label: Compare to mapping
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed indication to the documented mappings: Solid Blue = tipper is paused; Solid Green = tipping in process; Solid Red = Estop is activated; Solid White = AGV is present (post-tipping); Solid Red, Yellow, Green = RMS error; Flashing Blue = tipper is disabled; Flashing Green = tipper is "starved"; Flashing Red = tipper is faulted; Flashing White = SurePost tote is being sent to the station; Flashing Yellow = AGV is present (pre-tipping); Flashing Green, Yellow, Green with long horn alarm = sort is starting; Flashing White with intermittent horn alarm = bagout is complete.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Stacklight indicator reference while comparing observed color and flashing pattern.
Alarm list and timestamps if additional alarm context is needed while interpreting fault-related or horn-related indications.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed stacklight or horn pattern does not match any documented indication.
The user is being asked to take corrective action not supported by this status-only mapping.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Operator stations have two stacklights located at the top of either side of the frame. The stacklights provide information about the current status of the corresponding tipper and station, with listed solid and flashing color meanings and horn patterns. Solid Blue = paused; Solid Green = tipping in process; Solid Red = Estop activated; Solid White = AGV present post-tipping; Solid Red, Yellow, Green = RMS error; Flashing Blue = disabled; Flashing Green = starved; Flashing Red = faulted; Flashing White = SurePost tote being sent; Flashing Yellow = AGV present pre-tipping; Flashing Green, Yellow, Green with long horn = sort starting; Flashing White with intermittent horn = bagout complete.

steps.title: Record or communicate the documented status
steps.purpose: Ensure the interpreted status is conveyed for operational awareness.
steps.instruction: Record or communicate the documented status meaning for the corresponding tipper or station.
steps.expected_result: The documented status meaning is recorded or communicated accurately.
steps.healthy_condition: The communicated or recorded status matches the documented mapping that was observed.
steps.failure_condition: The status is not recorded or communicated, or it is communicated inaccurately.

actions.label: Record or communicate status
actions.exact_action: Record or communicate the documented status meaning for the corresponding tipper or station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Alarm timestamps if needed to support communication of current alarm-related status context.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed indication could not be matched to a documented status.
There is uncertainty about the correct documented meaning to communicate.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The stacklights provide information about the current status of the corresponding tipper and station.

success_criteria: The user determines the documented current status of the corresponding tipper or station from the observed stacklight and horn pattern.
The observed indication is matched to one documented mapping exactly.
The documented status meaning is recorded or communicated accurately.
healthy_conditions: The operator station stacklights are visible.
The indication can be distinguished as solid or flashing.
The displayed color or color combination is identifiable.
Any horn alarm pattern can be identified as present, long, or intermittent.
The observed indication matches one documented status mapping.
failure_conditions: The stacklights cannot be located or observed clearly.
The indication cannot be reliably distinguished as solid or flashing.
The color pattern or horn pattern cannot be determined confidently.
The observed stacklight or horn pattern does not match any documented indication.
A user attempts to infer corrective action from a status-only mapping.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed stacklight or horn pattern does not match any documented indication.
Escalate if the stacklight or horn pattern cannot be observed clearly enough to classify.
Do not infer corrective actions from the status mapping when the source provides status meaning only.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference image for stacklight hardware and indicator appearance.
VISU_ALARM screen showing tipper alarms and timestamps for related alarm context.
